meaning: adj.不科学的,无科学知识的,非学术的
examples: No member of the team was medically qualified and its methods were considered totally unscientific.
They say that portraying introduced species as inherently bad is an unscientific approach.
To show enthusiasm is to risk appearing unscientific, unobjective; it is to appeal to the students' emotions rather than their intellect.
Over the past several weeks I have used Facebook to undertake an unscientific experiment on branding.
We carry around a world of knowing that is totally private, remarkably unscientific and extremely powerful.
